data.title: code-reviewer-light used with Sonnet model in host project — light agent is designed for Haiku only
data.description:
## What happened

In a host project, the code-reviewer-light agent was dispatched with the Sonnet model. The light-tier agent is designed specifically for Haiku — it uses a simplified, single-pass checklist optimized for Haiku's capabilities and cost profile. When paired with Sonnet, the light agent's simplified checklist underutilizes Sonnet's analysis capability while still producing a review that appears to have Sonnet-level authority.

## Expected behavior

If the model tier is Sonnet or higher, the standard reviewer (dso:code-reviewer-standard) should be used, not the light reviewer. The light reviewer exists specifically for the Haiku cost/capability tier. Using code-reviewer-light with Sonnet is a tier mismatch — it gives a Sonnet-cost review with a Haiku-level analysis depth.

## Fix needed

1. The review dispatch logic in REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md Step 4 should enforce: if model_override is sonnet or opus, use at least the standard reviewer agent regardless of classifier tier
2. Alternatively, the light reviewer agent definition should include a guard that rejects non-Haiku model invocations
3. Document the light-agent = Haiku-only invariant in classifier-tier-output.md
